The rain came down in sheets, hammering the tin roof like a thousand tiny drummers with something to prove. She stood at the window, watching the garden dissolve into mud and memory. There was something about storms that made her feel both terrified and alive — a contradiction she had long since stopped trying to resolve. Her grandmother used to say that rain was just the sky remembering how to cry. She never understood that until now. The puddles forming on the walkway reflected a world turned upside down, and she thought maybe that was closer to the truth than anyone wanted to admit.
